text: The weights of 4 boxes are 90, 30, 20, and 50 kilograms. Which of the following cannot be the total weight, in kilograms, of any combination of these boxes, and in combination a box can be used only once?

Select the odd group of numbers. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g.13 Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
Three of the following four-letter clusters are alike in a certain way and one is different. Pick the odd one out.

The weights of the four boxes are 90, 30, 40, and 60 kilograms. Which of the following cannot be the total weight, in kilograms, of any combination of these boxes, and in combination, a box can be used only once?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: Some stones are cubes. No cube is a glass. All bats are cubes. Conclusions: I. Some stones being bats is a possibility. II. No bat is a glass. III. All cubes are bats. IV. No stone is a glass.

options.text: Only Conclusions I, II and IV follow
Only Conclusions II and III follow
Only Conclusions I and II follow

text: How does the sunlight affect the atmosphere in the bookstore?

Why did the children gather in the village square?

options.text: They were preparing for a festival.
They were participating in the annual treasure hunt.
They were attending a school assembly.

text: What was Whiskers doing as the children were engrossed in deciphering clues?

options.text: Chasing other animals in the square.

text: How did Whiskers unintentionally become a part of the treasure hunt?

options.text: Whiskers pounced on fallen leaves and chased imaginary foes.

text: Why did an adult scold Whiskers during the treasure hunt?

options.text: Whiskers was perceived as disrupting the event.

text: What was the outcome of the scolding received by Whiskers?

options.text: Whiskers joined the children in the hunt.
Whiskers found a cozy spot to nap.
Whiskers slinked away, leaving the children to resume their quest.
Whiskers continued to play with the children.
